title: Vascular smooth muscle cell (VSMC) isolation from mouse aorta
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cr1m0","text":"This is a method for VSMC isolation from mouse aorta by an explant method adapted from Metz et al (2012).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Sacrifice mouse (10-week old, Balb/c) with an overdose intraperitoneal injection of sodium pentobarbital.
Dip the mouse into a beaker filled with 70% ethanol, fix into an operation tray, bring into laminar air flow.
With sterilized surgical instruments, open the mouse, dissect the thoracic aorta.
Wash the aorta in Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS).
Keep the aorta in a dish on ice, remove the connective tissue and the adventitia.
Transfer into a new dish with fresh HBSS.
Cut open the aorta longitudinally, remove the endothelial layers by gentle scrape with a sterile cotton swab.
Cut the aorta into 4 pieces (around 4 mm 2 each), wash in Dulbecco’s modified Eagle’s medium/Ham’s F12 (DMEM/F12) (Gibco).

Transfer the aorta pieces into a collagen-coated culture dish (35 mm), press with the luminal side down on the dish. Spread the aorta out over as large as possible. Prepare the collagen-coated culture dish in advance. Add 1-2 ml collagen, incubate 30-60 minutes at room temperature, rinse with PBS three times, keep the last PBS up to use.
Incubate for 5 min in a 37 o C, 5% CO 2 incubator in a tilting position. Undetermined, 5% CO2 incubator 00:05:00
Check and make sure the media explant attached to the dish. Prolonged the incubation for another 1-3 minutes if necessary. Be careful not to dry the media explant.

Very slowly add fresh medium supplemented with 20% fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Sigma) and antibiotics (70 mg/ ml penicillin and 50 mg/ml streptomycin) it just covers the aorta. Start with 100 μl up to 500 μl. Make sure not to disturb the attachment of explants to the dish.
Place the dish in the incubator and left undisturbed for 4 days.
At day 5, add fresh medium and then change every 3 days. Observe the cells that had migrated from the medial explant under a microscope when changing medium.
When the migrated cells from media explants are confluent, harvest with trypsinization.

Count the cells and pool cells from media explants from 3 mice into one dish (0.5-1x10 5 cells/35 mm dish), culture in 10%FBS/DMEM/F12 (complete medium) as needed thereafter for experiments. This is passage 1.
creator: Muthi Ikawati
